evals.prompt: Draft a spec for adding email/password login to an existing web app. Include testable requirements and a test strategy. Save it under docs/specs/ (or .cursor/docs/specs/ if that layout exists).
evals.expected_output: A structured specification with overview, functional requirements (preferably REQ-XXX), acceptance criteria, constraints, out of scope, and an automated test strategy.
assertions: Surfaces assumptions or asks clarifying questions before treating vague auth details as settled
Output includes a dedicated requirements section with multiple concrete items
Requirements use IDs or otherwise uniquely referenceable items
Acceptance criteria are present in Given/When/Then or equivalent testable form
A test strategy section maps requirements to automated checks
Out of scope or constraints are explicitly listed

evals.prompt: We have a messy payments module with no docs. Walk me through the brownfield workflow before we change refund behavior — what do we do first and what artifacts should exist?
evals.expected_output: A clear sequence that starts with documenting current behavior (extract/baseline spec), then a change/delta spec, then plan/implement/review sized appropriately — not jumping straight to coding refunds.
assertions: Mentions documenting or extracting current behavior before implementing the change
Distinguishes baseline/current spec from the desired change spec
Mentions planning and/or review for non-trivial change
Does not recommend skipping straight to production code without a spec for this scenario

evals.prompt: hey can you just rename a typo in a comment in README — do we need the whole spec workflow?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes trivial scope and advises skipping the formal SDD workflow; handle in chat / direct edit.
assertions: Recommends skipping formal draft-spec/plan/implement workflow for this trivial change
Does not require producing a multi-section specification for a comment typo

evals.prompt: Create an implementation plan for a medium-sized feature whose spec already lists 6 REQ items touching API + UI + DB. Assume the spec file is at docs/specs/notifications.md.
evals.expected_output: An ordered plan with concrete tasks, files to touch, tests per requirement slice, and risks — suitable for medium size (plan before implement). Saved under docs/plans/ because the project already uses docs/specs/.
assertions: Plan is broken into ordered tasks or steps
Mentions specific areas or files (API, UI, DB or equivalents)
Includes testing or verification steps tied to requirements
Flags risks or dependencies
Uses docs/plans/ (or equivalent portable home) rather than forcing .cursor/plans/ when docs/specs/ is the existing convention

evals.prompt: We are in Cursor. /plan-impl a medium auth feature whose spec has REQ-001..REQ-009. Save under .cursor/plans/. The Plan UI must show real todos, not zero.
evals.expected_output: A .cursor/plans/*.plan.md file with YAML frontmatter containing a non-empty todos array (T1..Tn mapped to REQs), plus a body that may include a requirements→tasks table.
assertions: Plan file includes YAML frontmatter with a todos array
todos array is non-empty with id and content (and status) per task
Todo contents or body map tasks to REQ-XXX identifiers
Does not rely on a body-only requirements table without frontmatter todos

evals.prompt: Draft a spec for password reset in a skills-only app repo that has .agents/skills/ but no .cursor/commands or .cursor/docs tree. Where do you save the spec?
evals.expected_output: Spec written under docs/specs/ (portable default), not .cursor/docs/specs/.
assertions: Chooses docs/specs/ as the output home
Does not require creating .cursor/docs/specs/ for this skills-only layout

evals.prompt: Medium feature: add webhook delivery retries with exponential backoff. Before writing the full requirements body, what should you confirm about how we'll test this? Also — should we install mattpocock to-prd for the product requirements doc?
evals.expected_output: Sketches preferred test seams (highest useful existing boundary, e.g. outbound HTTP / queue consumer) and confirms them; rejects installing to-prd/to-spec alongside ADSK — use ADSK SDD for the engineering spec (and product-value-loop if discovery PRDs are needed).
assertions: Mentions test seams or preferred external boundaries before or while drafting
Prefers existing high-level seams over inventing many new internal doubles
Does not recommend installing to-prd or to-spec as the ADSK/product PRD solution
Points to ADSK spec-driven workflow (and optionally product-value-loop for discovery) instead of a parallel SDD pack
